allow:
Bash(git commit -m "$\(cat <<''EOF''
fix: Windows Playwright subprocess event loop issue - use async Playwright directly

- Replace ProcessPoolExecutor with direct async Playwright usage in _generate_computer_use_script
- This eliminates the Windows greenlet/event loop policy issue that was causing NotImplementedError
- Removes need for subprocess and _init_worker_process
- Add _execute_action_async helper method for executing page actions
- Add defensive event loop policy setting in SyncComputerUseService.__enter__
- Add CLAUDE.md with comprehensive project documentation

On Windows, sync_playwright with greenlets in subprocess contexts causes:
"asyncio.subprocess_exec raises NotImplementedError" because the event loop
policy needs to be set before the greenlet context is created. By using
async Playwright directly in the main async context, we avoid this issue entirely.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")

Bash(git commit -m "$\(cat <<''EOF''
fix: Windows Playwright event loop policy conflict - use ThreadPoolExecutor with ProactorEventLoopPolicy

Root cause: FastAPI uses WindowsSelectorEventLoopPolicy which does NOT support subprocess
operations. Playwright needs to create browser subprocesses, so it fails with
NotImplementedError in asyncio.create_subprocess_exec.

Solution: Run Playwright in a ThreadPoolExecutor with WindowsProactorEventLoopPolicy:
- Main FastAPI context: uses WindowsSelectorEventLoopPolicy \(required by uvicorn/async fixtures\)
- Playwright thread: uses WindowsProactorEventLoopPolicy \(supports subprocesses\)
- Each thread gets its own event loop, no policy conflicts

The _run_playwright_in_thread wrapper:
- Sets ProactorEventLoopPolicy on Windows
- Creates new event loop for the thread
- Runs the async Playwright function in that isolated loop

This is the correct architectural fix for Windows Playwright + async frameworks.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")
